response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist software engineer with broad experience across web, mobile, and systems programming, but he does not meet the core mandatory requirements. His professional C++ experience is limited to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014–2015, far short of the required 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. He has no demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience, and no fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain exposure — RIDE Capital was retail investment administration built on Node/TypeScript, not investment banking. His low-latency DLang work shows relevant performance-engineering instincts, but the skill and domain gaps are too large for this role.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of requests/sec) at Dunnhumby using async I/O and caching
Strong engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and open-source authorship
Broad polyglot background with some C/C++ exposure (LabWindows/CVI, wave field synthesis thesis)
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, versus the mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain experience
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering and performance background
